description: List all unique tags used by the current user across all their tasks, with usage counts and colors

changes: Initial implementation for task tags feature

input_schema.description: No input parameters required. Returns tags for the authenticated user.

tags.description: Array of tag objects sorted by usage count (descending)

color.description: Hex color code generated from tag name (e.g., '#3B82F6')

count.description: Number of tasks with this tag

natural_language_examples.intent: List all tags the user has created
natural_language_examples.expected_behavior: Returns list of all unique tags with counts, sorted by usage

natural_language_examples.user_input: what tags do I use most?
natural_language_examples.intent: Show tags sorted by usage count
natural_language_examples.expected_behavior: Same as above - default sort is by count (descending)

natural_language_examples.user_input: how many tags do I have?
natural_language_examples.expected_behavior: Returns tag list, agent responds with 'total' count

error_handling.trigger: User has no tasks or no tasks with tags

response.note: Empty result is valid, not an error

performance_requirements.implementation_approach: Single PostgreSQL query with jsonb_array_elements_text and GROUP BY

security_requirements: User isolation: Only returns tags from user's own tasks

understanding_commands: User says 'show tags' / 'list tags' / 'my tags' → list_tags()
User asks about 'categories' → list_tags() (tags are categories)
response_formatting: Show top 5-10 most used tags: 'Your most used tags: work (12 tasks), urgent (8 tasks), personal (5 tasks)'
If many tags: 'You have {total} tags. Top 5: ...'
If no tags: 'You haven't added any tags yet. Add tags with: "add task buy milk, tags: shopping"'
Show colors as visual indicators: 'work (🔵), urgent (🔴), personal (🟢)' or use color names

database.query: SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(tags) AS tag, COUNT(*) AS count FROM tasks WHERE user_id = ? GROUP BY tag ORDER BY count DESC

color_generation.algorithm: MD5 hash of tag name → hex color → brightness adjustment
color_generation.deterministic: Same tag always produces same color
color_generation.accessibility: Minimum brightness enforced for readability (WCAG 2.1 AA)

test_cases.test_name: List tags for user with multiple tags
test_cases.setup: User has 3 tasks: task1=['work'], task2=['work', 'urgent'], task3=['personal']

test_cases.test_name: List tags for user with no tags
test_cases.setup: User has tasks but no tags

test_cases.test_name: List tags for user with no tasks

test_cases.setup: User has tag 'work' on 3 different tasks

expected_output.note: All 3 instances of 'work' should generate the same color
